<p style=3D"margin: 16px 0;">If you work at a private equity firm and it ha=
s reached its 25th anniversary, it will probably have a party to celebrate.=
 If it has a party, it will probably book a musical guest. If it books a mu=
sical guest and David Solomon is unavailable, it will probably book Flo Rid=
a. You will go to your private equity firm=E2=80=99s anniversary party and =
your boss will give a speech about the value you provide to limited partner=
s, and then she will say =E2=80=9Cand now, Flo Rida.=E2=80=9D And then Flo =
Rida will come out on stage, and you will perhaps, for just a second, think=
, =E2=80=9Cwell, why Flo Rida?=E2=80=9D And he will probably be thinking =
=E2=80=9Cwell, why this private equity firm?=E2=80=9D But that is just life=
 in modern capitalism, sometimes private equity firms celebrate significant=
 milestones and Flo Rida is there. It doesn=E2=80=99t have to make sense; i=
t just is.</p>
<p style=3D"margin: 16px 0;">Evan Osnos has=C2=A0an <a href=3D"https://link=
.mail.bloombergbusiness.com/click/31711038.259416/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmV3eW9ya=
2VyLmNvbS9tYWdhemluZS8yMDIzLzA2LzA1L2hvdy10by1oaXJlLWEtcG9wLXN0YXItZm9yLXlv=
dXItcHJpdmF0ZS1wYXJ0eQ/607f07ceef4b8524a319a23cB709a076f" itemprop=3D"Story=
Link" itemscope=3D"itemscope" target=3D"_blank" style=3D"color: #000000; te=
xt-decoration: none !important; border-bottom-width: 1px; border-bottom-col=
or: #000000; border-bottom-style: solid; background-color: #ccc;">extremely=
 delightful New Yorker article</a> about the private gig industry, in which=
 star and former-star and near-star musicians collect large checks for play=
ing short sets for the bar mitzvahs and corporate parties of the wealthy. =
=E2=80=9CIf you can say, =E2=80=98Hey, I=E2=80=99m going to go have a bad t=
ime for an afternoon, but it=E2=80=99s going to pay for my kid=E2=80=99s en=
tire college education,=E2=80=99 then that=E2=80=99s a trade-off I think mo=
st responsible adults will make,=E2=80=9D says one booking agent, correctly=
. Osnos follows Flo Rida, who =E2=80=9Cattained celebrity in 2008 with his =
song =E2=80=98Low,=E2=80=99 an admiring ode to a Rubenesque beauty on the d=
ance floor,=E2=80=9D to both a bar mitzvah and a private equity gig:</p>
<blockquote>
<p style=3D"margin: 16px 0;">On a crisp spring night on the southern tip of=
 Manhattan, Flo Rida was backstage once more. This time, he huddled in a da=
rkened hallway with his dancers and the d.j. and Int=E2=80=99l Nephew, thei=
r arms laced around one another, as they murmured a prayer. Then they lined=
 up in a loose stack behind the door that separated them from the stage.=C2=
=A0</p>
<p style=3D"margin: 16px 0;">On the other side, in a cavernous ballroom bat=
hed in purple light, about four hundred people with matching lanyards were =
celebrating the twenty-fifth anniversary of a private-equity firm. It was b=
usiness casual, with the occasional bow tie and ball gown. A sushi buffet r=
an the length of one wall. The venue, Casa Cipriani, occupies a restored Be=
aux-Arts ferry terminal that also includes a hotel and private club, which =
offers suites with cashmere-lined walls, easy access to a helicopter pad, a=
nd fastidious attention to service. Guests are asked to choose which type o=
f Italian bedding they prefer, cotton or linen.</p>
<p style=3D"margin: 16px 0;">At the lectern, two of the firm=E2=80=99s lead=
ers were holding forth. A gray-haired man in a gray plaid blazer praised hi=
s colleagues=E2=80=99 delivery of =E2=80=9Cindustry-leading returns for our=
 limited partners,=E2=80=9D and thanked attendees for various feats, includ=
ing =E2=80=9Cfive hundred million in proceeds at a 5.5x multiple on investm=
ent capital=E2=80=9D and enduring =E2=80=9Cawful six-hour golf rounds with =
me.=E2=80=9D As showtime approached, a partner in an elegant black-and-whit=
e gown told the crowd, =E2=80=9CWith pride in our hearts and sushi in our m=
ouths, let=E2=80=99s celebrate.=E2=80=9D</p>
<p style=3D"margin: 16px 0;">The partygoers had not been told who the headl=
ine entertainer would be, and some were quietly hoping for Springsteen. Whe=
n Flo charged out from backstage, there was a brief vacuum of silence. But =
he plunged ahead into =E2=80=9CGood Feeling,=E2=80=9D a thumping ode to suc=
cess, with references to a Gulfstream, a Bugatti, and a Maybach. (=E2=80=9C=
Talk like a winner, my chest to that sun / G5 dealer, U.S. to Taiwan.=E2=80=
=9D) The crowd began to fall in, and Flo showed a rowdier mode than he had =
at the bar mitzvah. =E2=80=9CWho wants some shots?=E2=80=9D he shouted, hol=
ding up bottles of vodka and tequila. There was a nervous titter; a gentlem=
an in a bow tie craned his neck for a look, and then some of the younger gu=
ests stepped forward to drink from bottles held in Flo=E2=80=99s outstretch=
ed hands. The mood in the room started to evolve fast.</p>
</blockquote>
<p style=3D"margin: 16px 0;">Springsteen! The main thing that I took away f=
rom this is that Flo Rida=E2=80=99s band includes =E2=80=9Ca younger rapper=
 known as Int=E2=80=99l Nephew,=E2=80=9D the greatest stage name I have eve=
r seen, but I will probably also use the =E2=80=9Cwith pride in our hearts =
and sushi in our mouths=E2=80=9D line in the future.</p>
